message:
fix(fr-command): match upstream Redis 7.2 error wording for ZSET score/weight/limit (br-frankenredis-uczv)

Aligns three error paths in `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs` with the
exact wording emitted by upstream `t_zset.c` in Redis 7.2 so command
clients (and conformance fixtures) get byte-for-byte identical error
strings:

1. `parse_score_bound` — used by `ZRANGEBYSCORE`, `ZRANGE BYSCORE`,
   `ZCOUNT`, and `ZRANGESTORE BYSCORE`. Previously emitted
   `CommandError::Store(ValueNotFloat)` for malformed UTF-8, malformed
   numeric, and NaN inputs. Upstream `zslParseRange` emits
   `"ERR min or max is not a float"` for all of these. Centralizes
   that mapping in a `bad()` closure so all three branches
   (UTF-8 decode, exclusive bound, inclusive bound) share the
   wording, and so NaN explicitly returns `bad()` instead of the
   generic store error.

2. `parse_zset_algebra_options` — used by `ZUNIONSTORE`, `ZINTERSTORE`,
   `ZDIFFSTORE`, `ZUNION`, `ZINTER`, `ZDIFF`. Previously returned
   `CommandError::InvalidInteger` for unparseable WEIGHTS arguments
   and a custom `"ERR weight value is not a float"` for NaN.
   Upstream `zunionInterDiffGenericCommand` emits the same float
   wording for both cases. Adds a `bad_weight()` closure so UTF-8
   decode failures, parse failures, and NaN all converge on
   `"ERR weight value is not a float"`.

3. `zintercard` LIMIT parsing — used by `ZINTERCARD`. Previously
   delegated to `parse_u64_arg` whose error wording does not match
   upstream. `t_zset.c::zinterCardCommand` validates LIMIT as a
   non-negative `long long` and emits
   `"ERR LIMIT can't be negative"` for both negative integers and
   non-numeric arguments. Switches to `parse_i64_arg`, mapping the
   parse error to that wording, and adds an explicit `< 0` check
   that emits the same string before casting to `u64`.

Pure error-message parity work: behavior on the success path is
unchanged, and the only code-paths affected are ones that already
returned an error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
